sprint.title: Auth v1: Convex Auth (Email + Google OAuth) + Verification + Forgot Password + Branded Emails
sprint.goal: Ship a production-ready, free-tier authentication system using Convex Auth: email/password, email OTP/magic link, Google OAuth; email verification (optional), forgot password flow, and branded transactional emails via Resend free tier.
definitionOfDone: Users can sign up/in via Email+Password, Email OTP/Magic Link, and Google OAuth
Password reset works end-to-end (request reset -> verify -> set new password)
Email verification supported for password signups (configurable gate)
All auth emails are branded and sent via Resend free tier
Auth UI is elder-friendly and covers error states (rate limit, invalid code, expired code)
User sessions persist correctly and protected routes work
Security basics: rate limiting, anti-enumeration messaging, webhook/secret handling
Automated tests cover the critical auth flows

sourceNotes.convexAuthCapabilities: Convex Auth supports OTP/magic links, OAuth (incl. Google), and passwords incl. reset + optional verification.

backlog.title: Select Convex Auth methods + configure providers (Password, Email OTP/Magic Link, Google OAuth)

methods: Email OTP or Magic Link (choose one default, keep the other optional)
constraints: Use Convex Auth (no paid auth provider)
Keep UI custom (Convex Auth doesn’t ship UI components)
notes: Convex Auth methods supported per docs.

acceptanceTests: Auth providers configured and runnable locally
Google OAuth callback completes and creates/links a user

backlog.title: Auth database model (user profile, auth identities, email verification status)

tables: users (app profile: name, createdAt, plan flags, deletion status placeholders)
auth identities handled by Convex Auth
constraints: No sensitive auth secrets stored in your tables
Profile table must be compatible with retention sprint (deletion fields)

acceptanceTests: User profile auto-created on first login

backlog.title: Build signup/login UI (email, password, OTP/magic link, Google) — elder-friendly

uiRules: Single column, big inputs, clear CTAs
Avoid jargon: 'Send code' / 'Continue' / 'Sign in with Google'
Anti-enumeration: show same message for unknown email
constraints: Do not require magic link if user uses password; allow both

acceptanceTests: All auth screens work on mobile/desktop
Sign-in persists session and redirects correctly

backlog.title: Email sending via Resend free tier + branded templates (verification, OTP/magic link, reset)

templates: Your login code (OTP) OR magic link
constraints: Respect Resend free tier caps (3,000/mo, 100/day, 1 domain)

acceptanceTests: Links/codes work and are not leaked in logs

backlog.title: Forgot password flow (request reset -> verify -> set new password) with safety checks

flow: User verifies and sets new password
security: Rate limit reset requests per email + IP
Do not reveal if email exists
Add tests to ensure reset verification is bound correctly (avoid cross-email misuse)
notes: Password reset flows are documented by Convex Auth.

backlog.title: Route protection + session handling across app (studio, uploads, billing)

behavior: After sign-in, redirect back to intended route

acceptanceTests: Protected pages cannot be accessed unauthenticated
Deep link redirect works after login

backlog.title: Auth QA + automated tests (email/password, OTP/magic link, Google OAuth, reset)

automation: Unit tests for email template rendering
Integration tests for password reset flow
UI tests for sign-in screen states
manualChecklist: Sign up with password -> verify email -> login

acceptanceTests: At least 6 tests added and passing

notes: Convex Auth supports OTP/magic links, OAuth, and passwords (incl. reset and optional email verification).
Resend free tier limits should be respected to keep costs at zero.
